weather.summary: Saturday: 13°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 11°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun activities for your 3-year-old! Get ready for outdoor adventures and creative explorations. I've found some fantastic events, including free activities at museums and parks, ensuring a memorable and budget-friendly weekend.

activities.title: Victoria Park Easter Activities
activities.description: Victoria Park is hosting a range of free, daily activities during the Easter holidays, including sports, fishing, and pond dipping. Check their Facebook page for a detailed schedule and booking information for activities that require it.
activities.whyItWorks: With varied activities suitable for toddlers, and plenty of space for running around, it's a great way to spend a day outdoors. The pond dipping will be especially engaging for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Bus: Take the 277 or 425 bus from Hackney to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the specific start points for each activity on the Victoria Park Facebook page.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: The Young V&A (formerly the Museum of Childhood) offers interactive galleries designed specifically for children, including areas for design, imagine, and play. It features a collection of toys and objects related to childhood.
activities.whyItWorks: The Mini Museum is a sensory space perfect for babies and toddlers. The interactive exhibits and toy collections will capture the imagination of a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green, a 2-minute walk to the museum.
transport.tip: The Bethnal Green tube stop is not accessible; Cambridge Heath overground station is a 5-minute walk but also not accessible.

activities.title: Library Club at Hackney Showroom
activities.description: The Hackney Showroom hosts a weekly Library Club with a reading corner and a diverse book selection.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed space for toddlers to engage with books and stories. The reading corner is perfect for quiet time.

transport.fromHackney: Bus: Several buses run along Queensbridge Road. Check local routes.
transport.tip: Check the Hackney Showroom website for term-time dates.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: The Discover Children's Story Centre offers two floors of immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds, as well as the Story Garden playground. They host Baby Storytelling sessions plus crafts and singalong time.
activities.whyItWorks: The immersive play spaces and storytelling sessions will engage a 3-year-old's imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Central Line to Stratford.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for Baby Storytelling sessions.

activities.title: Revel Puck Circus: The Glimmer Daze Gambit
activities.address: Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park, London, E20 2ST
activities.description: The Revel Puck Circus presents 'The Glimmer Daze Gambit' at Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Circus performances can be visually stimulating and entertaining for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Central Line to Stratford and walk to Olympic Park.
transport.tip: Check website for showtimes and ticket prices.

activities.title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail
activities.description: Follow The Very Hungry Caterpillar Trail at Walthamstow Wetlands.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun outdoor trail based on a beloved children's book. Walking and exploring nature will be great for a 3-year-old.

transport.fromHackney: Bus: Take a bus towards Walthamstow and then a short walk to the Wetlands.
transport.tip: Check the Walthamstow Wetlands website for details.

activities.title: Spitalfields Bonnet Parade
activities.description: Don your most imaginative headwear and join the parade through the market. Judging and prize-giving follow at 2pm.
activities.whyItWorks: A creative and festive event where children can express their creativity by designing and wearing a bonnet.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk.
transport.tip: Arrive early to participate in the parade.

activities.title: Old Spitalfields Market Arts and Crafts
activities.description: Artist LuluJoelle will run free drop-in arts and crafts daily.
activities.whyItWorks: A creative and hands-on activity suitable for toddlers. The drop-in nature allows flexibility.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then walk.
transport.tip: No booking required.

activities.title: Natural History Museum Easter Family Festival
activities.description: The Natural History Museum is hosting an Easter Family Festival with free family-friendly activities and events.
activities.whyItWorks: The museum's dinosaur exhibits and interactive displays are engaging for young children. The Easter Family Festival offers additional activities designed for families.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for specific festival activities and times.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: The Science Museum offers free exhibits exploring science and technology. The Garden is an interactive space for young children to discover construction, water, light, and sound through play.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden is designed for young children to explore scientific concepts through hands-on play. The space exploration gallery is also captivating.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: The Wonderlab offers a great hands-on experience for a fee.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Battersea Park offers gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo. It's a spacious park with lots to explore.
activities.whyItWorks: The playgrounds and open spaces are perfect for toddlers to run around and play. The mini zoo will be a hit with animal lovers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a short bus ride to Battersea Park.
transport.tip: Check out Battersea Children's Zoo for a dedicated animal experience (paid entry).

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Golders Hill Park Zoo is a small zoo in the Hampstead Heath area, home to rare species of birds and animals including wallabies and lemurs.
activities.whyItWorks: A free zoo is a great way to entertain a toddler. The animals are sure to capture their attention.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Northern Line to Golders Green.
transport.tip: Combine the zoo visit with a picnic in the park.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: The Diana Memorial Playground features a Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship, sensory trail, teepees, and plenty of seating.
activities.whyItWorks: The pirate ship and sensory trail offer a stimulating and fun play environment for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate or the District/Circle Line to High Street Kensington.
transport.tip: This playground is very popular, so expect crowds.

activities.title: Southbank Centre Spring Family Fun
activities.description: The Southbank Centre is hosting Spring Family Fun with shows, musical adventures, puppetry, and free events. Check the calendar for details.
activities.whyItWorks: The variety of activities caters to different interests. The free events make it accessible for families.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Jubilee Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for a detailed schedule of events and age recommendations.

activities.title: Architecture Explorers: Family Trail
activities.description: Explore the architecture of the Royal Festival Hall with this interactive family trail.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun and engaging way to explore the Southbank Centre and learn about architecture. The interactive nature will keep a 3-year-old interested.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the Jubilee Line to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Pick up the trail map at the Southbank Centre.

activities.title: Laaaunch!
activities.description: A multi-sensory, space-themed show for babies.
activities.whyItWorks: Multi-sensory experiences are great for engaging babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a train to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Check the Polka Theatre website for showtimes and ticket prices.

activities.title: Horniman Spring Fair
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: The Horniman Spring Fair features music, circus skill workshops, and a 'Smallympics'.
activities.whyItWorks: The variety of activities caters to different interests and age groups. The circus skills workshop will be particularly engaging.

transport.fromHackney: Overground: Take the Overground to Forest Hill.
transport.tip: Pre-booking is recommended.

activities.title: Hamleys' Easter Happenings
activities.description: Hamleys is hosting various Easter events, including a puppet show, rainbow ribbon parade, storytime, and an Easter egg hunt.
activities.whyItWorks: The toy store is a magical place for children. The Easter-themed events add to the excitement.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Oxford Circus.
transport.tip: Check the Hamleys website for a detailed schedule of events and ticket prices.

activities.title: Sounds of Blossom festival at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Explore the Sounds of Blossom festival at Kew Gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Kew Gardens offers a beautiful outdoor space for children to explore. The spring blossoms and themed activities will be particularly appealing.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the District Line to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Check the Kew Gardens website for details.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.address: Hampton Court, East Molesey, Surrey KT8 9AU
activities.description: Roam around the gardens and find hidden Lindt Gold Bunny statues to win a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: An Easter egg hunt in a historic palace is a memorable experience for children. The chocolate treat is a bonus.

transport.fromHackney: Train: Take a train from Waterloo to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially during the Easter holidays.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Search for hidden eggs around the zoo and uncover a secret code.
activities.whyItWorks: Combining an Easter egg hunt with a visit to the zoo is a fun and engaging activity for children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Regent's Park.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance, especially during the Easter holidays.

activities.title: Paddington Bear Experience Easter Event
activities.address: County Hall, Riverside Building, Westminster Bridge Rd, London SE1 7PB
activities.description: Enjoy an Easter makeover of the Paddington Bear Experience, including an Easter egg hunt around Windsor Gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: The immersive experience and themed Easter egg hunt will be a magical and memorable adventure for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Take the Jubilee Line to Waterloo or Westminster.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as slots fill up fast.
